title: 'Authentic' | Starbucks bets on employee-generated content with launch of TikTok scheme
tldr: Starbucks is formalizing employee-generated content with a dedicated TikTok program, betting on staff storytelling over polished brand ads. A concrete case study of EGC going mainstream at a major brand.
why_fresh: named EGC program launch at a flagship brand
angle: What Starbucks' TikTok EGC scheme teaches comms teams about trusting employees as creators versus controlling the narrative.

title: Unilever tells agencies to make way for creators: 'We don't need the big idea'
tldr: Unilever is pivoting budget from agency-led 'big ideas' to creator-driven content at scale, signaling a structural shift in how major advertisers build brand narratives. A major sign of where content power is moving.
why_fresh: major advertiser repudiating the 'big idea' model
angle: If Unilever doesn't need the big idea, what's left for brand storytellers — and is creator volume really a strategy?

title: Reddit sells itself as the human internet
tldr: Reddit's 'People Are The Best' campaign positions the platform as the home of authentic human conversation against an AI-saturated web. A clear bet on humanity-over-polish as a brand differentiator.
angle: As AI floods feeds, 'the human internet' becomes a marketing claim — what that means for content authenticity.

title: How Deutsche Welle Builds Audience Strategy Across 30-Plus Languages in the Age of Algorithmic Discovery
tldr: DW has dismantled the old 'publish, optimize, drive traffic back' playbook, embedding audience development inside the newsroom across 30+ languages. A deep case study in modern distribution-first strategy and transcreation at scale.
why_fresh: newsroom-embedded audience model across many languages
angle: What a 30-language newsroom teaches content strategists about transcreation and distribution-first thinking.

title: Mark Ritson: Gen Z didn't kill cinema, beer or soda. Tired marketing did
tldr: Ritson argues generational blame is a cop-out for stale marketing and weak brand-building, not shifting consumer habits. A provocative reframe of how we read media-consumption trends.
why_fresh: contrarian take on generational consumption myths
angle: Stop blaming Gen Z: a piece on how lazy audience narratives mislead content and brand strategy.

title: Koto reframes Stack Overflow around the one thing AI can't replace: its community
tldr: Faced with AI eroding its traffic, Stack Overflow rebrands around human community as its defensible value. A sharp case study in finding the human core when AI commoditizes your content.
angle: When AI eats your category, community becomes the moat — lessons from Stack Overflow's reframe.

title: The 3 kinds of attention, according to MIDiA
tldr: Creator analyst Ben Woods breaks attention into three distinct types and how to win the right kind on social platforms. A useful new framework for thinking past raw reach.
angle: Not all attention is equal: applying MIDiA's three-attention model to content planning.

title: Your Brand Story Is No Longer Yours Alone: Why AI Is Changing How Retail Narratives Are Formed
tldr: AI answer engines and synthesized summaries mean brands no longer fully control how their story is told. A timely look at narrative governance when machines mediate your message.
angle: Losing the last word: how comms teams keep narrative integrity when AI retells your brand story.

title: Cannes Lions 2026: why resonance, niche and analogue are the future of creativity
tldr: A Cannes recap argues reach is out and resonance, niche audiences and analogue craft are ascendant. A useful read on where creative and content priorities are shifting.
angle: Resonance over reach: what the niche-and-analogue turn means for content strategists chasing scale.

title: PR Roundup: Apple's Price Hike Playbook, Social Owns the Crisis Moment, and Starbucks Embraces Employee Storytelling
tldr: A roundup on how social now owns the crisis moment, Apple's pricing communications, and Starbucks' employee storytelling push. Practical signals for crisis comms and EGC.
why_fresh: routine roundup but with EGC/crisis angle
angle: Why social platforms — not press releases — now own the first hour of a crisis.

title: Creators are rethinking the exit
tldr: Top creators are reconsidering acquisitions and exit strategies, building durable media businesses rather than selling out. A signal of the creator economy maturing into real enterprises.
angle: From exit to empire: what creators going long-term means for brands partnering with them.

title: The Operating Model Behind Trustworthy Content at Scale
tldr: Contently lays out how to spot when a high-volume content program isn't actually landing, and the operating model needed for trustworthy content at scale. Practical for content ops leaders.
why_fresh: useful ops framing, not a new trend
angle: Volume isn't impact: diagnosing content programs that produce a lot but move nothing.

title: Saudi users more selective on social media as digital maturity rises: Deloitte
tldr: Deloitte finds Saudi users growing more selective and discerning on social media as digital maturity increases. A concrete data point on evolving media-consumption habits in a fast-growing market.
why_fresh: new consumption-habits data for a region
angle: Digital maturity makes audiences pickier — what selective social use means for content reach.

title: Capital Weather is dropping the 'Gang' and going independent
tldr: The Washington Post's beloved Capital Weather Gang spins out to go independent after 18 years. A case study in trusted niche brands leaving legacy outlets for owned, direct audiences.
why_fresh: trusted brand spins out of legacy media
angle: When the talent owns the audience: niche brands going independent and what it signals for media business models.

title: How great leaders create a shared identity
tldr: Fast Company explores how leaders forge a shared identity, using Brzezinski's read of Soviet fragility as a frame. Relevant to internal comms and corporate narrative building.
angle: Shared identity as the real job of internal comms — beyond mission statements.
